graph.theorem_statement: If a and b are nonnegative, then a + b is nonnegative.

nodes.title: Nonnegative sum
nodes.informal_statement: If 0 <= a and 0 <= b, then 0 <= a + b.
nodes.informal_proof_text: Assume 0 <= a and 0 <= b. It suffices to derive 0 <= a + b from these assumptions.
nodes.formalization_rationale: The main theorem is still very small and self-contained, with a short proof likely obtained immediately from the same arithmetic fact used in the intermediate step.

nodes.title: Assumptions
nodes.informal_proof_text: These are the hypotheses assumed in the proof.

nodes.title: Sum of nonnegative reals is nonnegative
nodes.informal_statement: If 0 <= x and 0 <= y, then 0 <= x + y.
nodes.informal_proof_text: This is the standard arithmetic lemma invoked in the proof.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Technically clean and self-contained, but lower priority for a first local prototype because it is a general library-style arithmetic lemma that Lean likely already provides rather than something that needs bespoke local formalization.

formal_artifact.lean_statement: theorem nonnegative_sum {x y : ℝ} (hx : 0 <= x) (hy : 0 <= y) : 0 <= x + y
formal_artifact.lean_code:
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

 theorem nonnegative_sum {x y : ℝ} (hx : 0 <= x) (hy : 0 <= y) : 0 <= x + y := by
  exact add_nonneg hx hy

nodes.title: Derived nonnegativity of the sum
nodes.informal_proof_text: Apply the standard lemma on sums of nonnegative reals to the assumptions 0 <= a and 0 <= b.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Directly local and low-dependency: it is the concrete goal under the current hypotheses and should formalize as a one-step application of a standard nonnegativity lemma.

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: The theorem is established once the desired conclusion 0 <= a + b has been derived from the assumptions.

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: The derivation uses the assumed facts 0 <= a and 0 <= b.

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: The derivation applies the standard lemma that the sum of two nonnegative reals is nonnegative.
